summary: Typosquat of @solana/spl-token with bundled child_process + network exfiltration patterns
details: Package name `@solana-labs/spl-toke` is a clear typosquat of the legitimate `@solana/spl-token` (missing trailing `n`, scope renamed to `@solana-labs`). The shipped bundles (lib/index.cjs.js, lib/index.esm.js) contain the suspicious composition of `require('child_process')`, hardcoded `curl`/`ping` shell invocations, and outbound `fetch`/POST calls colocated within the same minified module — a fingerprint inconsistent with the legitimate spl-token client library, which is a pure JS RPC wrapper with no shell or process-spawning surface. The combination of (a) ≤2-character edit from a top-tier registry package in a high-value ecosystem (Solana token tooling, frequent target for credential and wallet theft) and (b) bundled child_process + network primitives that have no place in the advertised API meets the typosquat-with-payload bar.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
